title: Emma Raducanu Skips Another Tennis Tournament
summary: Emma Raducanu is a famous tennis player from Britain who won the US Open when she was a teenager. She has not played a match since early March because she has been sick with a viral illness. Now, she has decided not to play in the Madrid Open tennis tournament, which means she will miss more games. Raducanu needs time to get better and practice on clay courts, which are a type of tennis surface that can be hard on her back. She has had back problems before on clay. Her next chance to play on clay might be in Rome in May. If she doesn't play soon, she might have a long break from tennis, which could make it tough for her when she tries to play on grass courts later in the year. Raducanu is working on her fitness and hopes to return strong.

background_read: Emma Raducanu is a British tennis player who became famous by winning the US Open in 2021 when she was just 18 years old. Tennis is a popular sport played on different surfaces like clay, grass, and hard courts. Players often get sick or injured and need time to recover before they can compete again. Tournaments like the Madrid Open are important events where top players from around the world compete for titles. Staying fit and healthy is key for athletes to perform their best in sports.
Article_Structure: WHO: Emma Raducanu, a British tennis player and former US Open winner
WHAT: She is missing the Madrid Open due to illness and needs time to recover and train on clay courts
WHERE: The Madrid Open in Spain and potentially the Italian Open in Rome
WHY: To avoid further injury and prepare properly for future tournaments on clay and grass courts

perspectives.description: Focusing on Raducanu's need to prioritize her health and fitness over playing tournaments to prevent injuries.
